items.title: Public PoC Released for Critical libssh2 CVE-2026-55200 Client-Side SSH Flaw
top_recommendations: Map affected product versions from the article to the software actually deployed in your environment.
If no vendor patch is available, implement temporary mitigations such as WAF rules, strict access control, and additional segmentation.

items.title: Why Post-Quantum Cryptography Starts With Credentials
top_recommendations: Run targeted awareness for users most likely to be impacted by the described phishing templates.
Review MFA policies and disable legacy authentication protocols (POP/IMAP/SMTP basic auth and other non‑MFA flows).

items.title: Data breach exposes up to 14.2 million email logins at six ISPs
top_recommendations: Run an out-of-band malware scan on servers and endpoints, focusing on recent changes.
Collect suspicious binaries from Temp/AppData and submit them to a sandbox or reverse engineering pipeline.

items.title: Ukraine Says Russian Intelligence Used Fake Support Texts to Steal Messaging Credentials
top_recommendations: Run an out-of-band malware scan on servers and endpoints, focusing on recent changes.
Collect suspicious binaries from Temp/AppData and submit them to a sandbox or reverse engineering pipeline.

items.title: Third-Party Breaches Teach Education Sector a Costly Lesson in Vendor Risk
top_recommendations: Validate the integrity and recoverability of recent backups for all critical systems.
Review exposed RDP/VPN entry points and restrict access using MFA and network segmentation.
